intended_use: Careverse CoronaryDoc is a web-based software application that is intended to be used by trained medical professionals as an interactive tool for viewing and analyzing cardiac computed tomography (CT)
intended_use_full: Careverse CoronaryDoc is a web-based software application that is intended to be used by trained medical professionals as an interactive tool for viewing and analyzing cardiac computed tomography (CT) data for determining the presence and extent of coronary plaques (i.e., atherosclerosis) and stenosis in patients who underwent Coronary Computed Tomography Angiography (CCTA) for evaluation of CAD or suspected CAD. This software post processes CT images obtained using any Computed Tomography (CT) scanner. The software provides tools for the measurement and visualization of coronary arteries.
target_condition: Coronary artery disease (CAD) or suspected CAD; coronary plaques (atherosclerosis) and stenosis
ground_truth: US expert readers (ground truth results for comparison)
other_metrics: Dice Coefficient: 0.899; 95% Hausdorff Distance: 4.366; Stenosis grade agreement: non-stenosis 98.97%, minor stenosis 91.25%, minimal stenosis 87.00%, moderate stenosis 87.23%, severe stenosis 83.72%, complete occlusion 91.18%; Plaque performance - Pearson Correlation: vessel volume 98.42%, lumen volume 98.52%, total plaque volume 96.94%, calcified plaque volume 97.92%, non-calcified plaque volume 95.14%, low-density non-calcified 88.42%; Bland-Altman Agreement: vessel volume 95.18%, lumen volume 94.78%, total plaque volume 95.91%, calcified plaque volume 98.00%, non-calcified plaque volume 95.24%, low-density non-calcified 94.96%
